# hmmlearn

{{ GitHub }}

 

{{ PyPI }}

{{ Read the Docs }}

 

{{ Build }}

 

{{ CodeCov }}

hmmlearn is a set of algorithms for **unsupervised** learning and inference
of Hidden Markov Models. For supervised learning learning of HMMs and similar
models see [seqlearn].

**Note**: This package is under limited-maintenance mode.

# Important links

- Official source code repo: <https://github.com/hmmlearn/hmmlearn>
- HTML documentation (stable release): <https://hmmlearn.readthedocs.org/en/stable>
- HTML documentation (development version): <https://hmmlearn.readthedocs.org/en/latest>

# Dependencies

The required dependencies to use hmmlearn are

- Python >= 3.6
- NumPy >= 1.10
- scikit-learn >= 0.16

You also need Matplotlib >= 1.1.1 to run the examples and pytest >= 2.6.0 to run
the tests.

# Installation

Requires a C compiler and Python headers.

To install from PyPI:

```
pip install --upgrade --user hmmlearn
```

To install from the repo:

```
pip install --user git+https://github.com/hmmlearn/hmmlearn
```

[seqlearn]: https://github.com/larsmans/seqlearn

functions.name: hmmlearn.utils.normalize
documentation:
Normalize the input array so that it sums to 1.

Parameters
----------
a : array
    Non-normalized input data.

axis : int
    Dimension along which normalization is performed.

Notes
-----
Modifies the input **inplace**.

functions.name: hmmlearn.utils.log_normalize
documentation:
Normalize the input array so that `sum(exp(a)) == 1`.

# Parameters

a

: Non-normalized input data.

axis

: Dimension along which normalization is performed.

# Notes

Modifies the input **inplace**.

modules.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence
documentation:
All implementations are based upon the following:
    http://www.fil.ion.ucl.ac.uk/~wpenny/publications/densities.ps

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence.kl_dirichlet
documentation:
KL Divergence between two dirichlet distributions

KL(q || p) = ln [gamma(q)/gamma(p)] - sum [ ln [gamma(q_j)/gamma(p_j)]
             - (q_j - p_j) (digamma(q_j) - digamma(p_j)]

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence.kl_normal_distribution
documentation: KL Divergence between two normal distributions.

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence.kl_multivariate_normal_distribution
documentation:
KL Divergence of two Multivariate Normal Distribtuions

q(x) = Normal(x; mean_q, variance_q)
p(x) = Normal(x; mean_p, variance_p)

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence.kl_gamma_distribution
documentation:
KL Divergence between two gamma distributions

q(x) = Gamma(x; b_q, c_q)
p(x) = Gamma(x; b_p, c_p)

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence.kl_wishart_distribution
documentation:
KL Divergence between two Wishart Distributions

q(x) = Wishart(R|dof_q, scale_q)
p(x) = Wishart(R|dof_p, scale_p)

Definition from:
    Shihao Ji, B. Krishnapuram, and L. Carin,
    "Variational Bayes for continuous hidden Markov models and its
    application to active learning," IEEE Transactions on Pattern
    Analysis and Machine Intelligence, vol. 28, no. 4, pp. 522–532,
    Apr. 2006, doi: 10.1109/TPAMI.2006.85.

functions.name: hmmlearn._kl_divergence._E
documentation: $L(a, B) = \int \mathcal{Wishart}(\Gamma; a, B) \log |\Gamma| d\Gamma$

functions.name: hmmlearn.stats.log_multivariate_normal_density
documentation:
Compute the log probability under a multivariate Gaussian distribution.

Parameters
----------
X : array_like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
    List of n_features-dimensional data points. Each row corresponds to a
    single data point.

means : array_like, shape (n_components, n_features)
    List of n_features-dimensional mean vectors for n_components Gaussians.
    Each row corresponds to a single mean vector.

covars : array_like
    List of n_components covariance parameters for each Gaussian. The shape
    depends on `covariance_type`:

    * (n_components, )                        if "spherical",
    * (n_components, n_features)              if "diag",
    * (n_components, n_features, n_features)  if "full",
    * (n_features, n_features)                if "tied".

covariance_type : {"spherical", "diag", "full", "tied"}, optional
    The type of the covariance parameters.  Defaults to 'diag'.

Returns
-------
lpr : array_like, shape (n_samples, n_components)
    Array containing the log probabilities of each data point in
    X under each of the n_components multivariate Gaussian distributions.

functions.name: hmmlearn.tests.test_gmm_hmm_multisequence.test_gmmhmm_multi_sequence_fit_invariant_to_sequence_ordering
documentation:
Sanity check GMM-HMM fit behaviour when run on multiple sequences
aka multiple frames.

Training data consumed during GMM-HMM fit is packed into a single
array X containing one or more sequences. In the case where
there are two or more input sequences, the ordering that the
sequences are packed into X should not influence the results
of the fit. Major differences in convergence during EM
iterations by merely permuting sequence order in the input
indicates a likely defect in the fit implementation.

Note: the ordering of samples inside a given sequence
is very meaningful, permuting the order of samples would
destroy the the state transition structure in the input data.

See issue 410 on github:
https://github.com/hmmlearn/hmmlearn/issues/410

classes.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalCategoricalHMM

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalCategoricalHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states.

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

emissionprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`emissionprob_`.

n_features: int, optional

: The number of categorical symbols in the HMM.  Will be inferred
  from the data if not set.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any
  combination of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, and 'e' for
  emissionprob.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalCategoricalHMM._init
documentation:
Initialize model parameters prior to fitting.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

Validate model parameters prior to fitting.

# Raises

ValueError

: If any of the parameters are invalid, e.g. if {attr}`startprob_`
  don't sum to 1.

Perform the M-step of the VB-EM algorithm.

Parameters

    Sufficient statistics updated from all available samples.

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalCategoricalHMM._compute_lower_bound
documentation: Compute the lower bound of the model.

Hidden Markov Model with categorical (discrete) emissions trained
using Variational Inference.

References:
: - <https://cse.buffalo.edu/faculty/mbeal/thesis/>

# Attributes

n_features

: Number of possible symbols emitted by the model (in the samples).

[monitor]

: Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.

[startprob_prior]

: Prior for the initial state occupation distribution.

[startprob_posterior]

: Posterior estimate of the state occupation distribution.

[transmat_prior]

: Prior for the matrix of transition probabilities between states.

[transmat_posterior]

: Posterior estimate of the transition probabilities between states.

[emissionprob_prior]

: Prior estimatate of emitting a given symbol when in each state.

[emissionprob_posterior]

: Posterior estimate of emitting a given symbol when in each state.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from hmmlearn.hmm import VariationalCategoricalHMM
>>> VariationalCategoricalHMM(n_components=2)  #doctest: +ELLIPSIS
VariationalCategoricalHMM(algorithm='viterbi',...
```

classes.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalGaussianHMM

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalGaussianHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states.

covariance_type

: The type of covariance parameters to use:

  - "spherical" --- each state uses a single variance value that
    applies to all features (default).
  - "diag" --- each state uses a diagonal covariance matrix.
  - "full" --- each state uses a full (i.e. unrestricted)
    covariance matrix.
  - "tied" --- all states use **the same** full covariance matrix.

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

means_prior, beta_prior

: Mean and precision of the Normal prior distribtion for
  {attr}`means_`.

scale_prior, dof_prior

: Parameters of the prior distribution for the covariance matrix
  {attr}`covars_`.

  If {attr}`covariance_type` is "spherical" or "diag" the prior is
  the inverse gamma distribution, otherwise --- the inverse Wishart
  distribution.

  The shape of the scale_prior array depends on
  {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - (n_components, )                        if "spherical",
  - (n_components, n_features)              if "diag",
  - (n_components, n_features, n_features)  if "full",
  - (n_features, n_features)                if "tied".

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any combination
  of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, 'm' for means, and 'c' for
  covars.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalGaussianHMM.covars_
documentation: Return covars as a full matrix.

Compat for _BaseGaussianHMM.  We return the mean of the
approximating distribution, which for us is just `means_posterior_`

# Parameters

methods.name: hmmlearn.vhmm.VariationalGaussianHMM._do_mstep
documentation:
Perform the M-step of VB-EM algorithm.

Parameters
----------
stats : dict
    Sufficient statistics updated from all available samples.

Hidden Markov Model with Multivariate Gaussian Emissions trained
using Variational Inference.

References:
: - <https://arxiv.org/abs/1605.08618>
  - <https://core.ac.uk/reader/10883750>
  - <https://theses.gla.ac.uk/6941/7/2005McGroryPhD.pdf>

# Attributes

n_features

: Dimensionality of the Gaussian emissions.

[monitor]

: Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.

[startprob_prior]

: Prior for the initial state occupation distribution.

[startprob_posterior]

: Posterior estimate of the state occupation distribution.

[transmat_prior]

: Prior for the matrix of transition probabilities between states.

[transmat_posterior]

: Posterior estimate of the transition probabilities between states.

[means_prior]: array, shape (n_components, n_features)

: Prior estimates for the mean of each state.

[means_posterior]: array, shape (n_components, n_features)

: Posterior estimates for the mean of each state.

[beta_prior]: array, shape (n_components, )

: Prior estimate on the scale of the variance over the means.

[beta_posterior]: array, shape (n_components, )

: Posterior estimate of the scale of the variance over the means.

[covars]

: Covariance parameters for each state.

  The shape depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - (n_components, )                        if "spherical",
  - (n_components, n_features)              if "diag",
  - (n_components, n_features, n_features)  if "full",
  - (n_features, n_features)                if "tied".

[dof_prior]: int / array

: The Degrees Of Freedom prior for each state's Wishart distribution.
  The type depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - array, shape (n_components, )  if "full",
  - int                            if "tied".

[dof_prior]: int / array

: The Prior on the Degrees Of Freedom
  for each state's Wishart distribution.
  The type depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - array, shape (n_components, )  if "full",
  - int                            if "tied".

[dof_posterior]: int / array

: The Degrees Of Freedom for each state's Wishart distribution.
  The type depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - array, shape (n_components, )  if "full",
  - int                            if "tied".

[scale_prior]

: Prior for the Inverse scale parameter for each state's
  Wishart distribution. The wishart distribution is
  the conjugate prior for the covariance.

  The shape depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - (n_components, )                        if "spherical",
  - (n_components, n_features)              if "diag",
  - (n_components, n_features, n_features)  if "full",
  - (n_features, n_features)                if "tied".

[scale_posterior]

: Inverse scale parameter for each state's wishart distribution.
  The wishart distribution is the conjugate prior for the covariance.

  The shape depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - (n_components, )                        if "spherical",
  - (n_components, n_features)              if "diag",
  - (n_components, n_features, n_features)  if "full",
  - (n_features, n_features)                if "tied".

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from hmmlearn.hmm import VariationalGaussianHMM
>>> VariationalGaussianHMM(n_components=2)  #doctest: +ELLIPSIS
VariationalGaussianHMM(algorithm='viterbi',...
```

# Parameters

n_components

  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

emissionprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`emissionprob_`.

n_features: int, optional

: The number of categorical symbols in the HMM.  Will be inferred
  from the data if not set.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to

  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any
  combination of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, and 'e' for
  emissionprob.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

methods.name: hmmlearn.hmm.GaussianHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states.

covariance_type

: The type of covariance parameters to use:

  - "spherical" --- each state uses a single variance value that
    applies to all features (default).
  - "diag" --- each state uses a diagonal covariance matrix.
  - "full" --- each state uses a full (i.e. unrestricted)
    covariance matrix.
  - "tied" --- all states use **the same** full covariance matrix.

min_covar

: Floor on the diagonal of the covariance matrix to prevent
  overfitting. Defaults to 1e-3.

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

means_prior, means_weight

: Mean and precision of the Normal prior distribtion for
  {attr}`means_`.

covars_prior, covars_weight

: Parameters of the prior distribution for the covariance matrix
  {attr}`covars_`.

  If {attr}`covariance_type` is "spherical" or "diag" the prior is
  the inverse gamma distribution, otherwise --- the inverse Wishart
  distribution.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any combination
  of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, 'm' for means, and 'c' for
  covars.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

classes.name: hmmlearn.hmm.GMMHMM

methods.name: hmmlearn.hmm.GMMHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states in the model.

n_mix

: Number of states in the GMM.

covariance_type

: The type of covariance parameters to use:

  - "spherical" --- each state uses a single variance value that
    applies to all features.
  - "diag" --- each state uses a diagonal covariance matrix
    (default).
  - "full" --- each state uses a full (i.e. unrestricted)
    covariance matrix.
  - "tied" --- all mixture components of each state use **the same**
    full covariance matrix (note that this is not the same as for
    `GaussianHMM`).

min_covar

: Floor on the diagonal of the covariance matrix to prevent
  overfitting. Defaults to 1e-3.

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

weights_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`weights_`.

means_prior, means_weight

: Mean and precision of the Normal prior distribtion for
  {attr}`means_`.

covars_prior, covars_weight

: Parameters of the prior distribution for the covariance matrix
  {attr}`covars_`.

  If {attr}`covariance_type` is "spherical" or "diag" the prior is
  the inverse gamma distribution, otherwise --- the inverse Wishart
  distribution.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any combination
  of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, 'm' for means, 'c'
  for covars, and 'w' for GMM mixing weights.  Defaults to all
  parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

Hidden Markov Model with Gaussian mixture emissions.

# Attributes

[monitor]

: Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.

[startprob]

: Initial state occupation distribution.

[transmat]

: Matrix of transition probabilities between states.

[weights]

: Mixture weights for each state.

[means]

: Mean parameters for each mixture component in each state.

[covars]

: Covariance parameters for each mixture components in each state.

  The shape depends on {attr}`covariance_type`:

  - (n_components, n_mix)                          if "spherical",
  - (n_components, n_mix, n_features)              if "diag",
  - (n_components, n_mix, n_features, n_features)  if "full"
  - (n_components, n_features, n_features)         if "tied".

classes.name: hmmlearn.hmm.MultinomialHMM

methods.name: hmmlearn.hmm.MultinomialHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states.

n_trials

: Number of trials (when sampling, all samples must have the same
  {attr}`n_trials`).

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any
  combination of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, and 'e' for
  emissionprob.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

Hidden Markov Model with multinomial emissions.

# Attributes

n_features

: Number of possible symbols emitted by the model (in the samples).

[monitor]

: Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.

[startprob]

: Initial state occupation distribution.

[transmat]

: Matrix of transition probabilities between states.

[emissionprob]

: Probability of emitting a given symbol when in each state.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from hmmlearn.hmm import MultinomialHMM
```

methods.name: hmmlearn.hmm.PoissonHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states.

startprob_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for
  {attr}`startprob_`.

transmat_prior

: Parameters of the Dirichlet prior distribution for each row
  of the transition probabilities {attr}`transmat_`.

lambdas_prior, lambdas_weight

: The gamma prior on the lambda values using alpha-beta notation,
  respectivley. If None, will be set based on the method of
  moments.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any
  combination of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, and 'l' for
  lambdas.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.

Hidden Markov Model with Poisson emissions.

Attributes
----------
monitor_ : ConvergenceMonitor
    Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.

startprob_ : array, shape (n_components, )
    Initial state occupation distribution.

transmat_ : array, shape (n_components, n_components)
    Matrix of transition probabilities between states.

lambdas_ : array, shape (n_components, n_features)
    The expectation value of the waiting time parameters for each
    feature in a given state.

variables.name: hmmlearn.base._BaseHMM
documentation: Backcompat name, will be deprecated in the future.

classes.name: hmmlearn.base.ConvergenceMonitor

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.ConvergenceMonitor.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
tol : double
    Convergence threshold.  EM has converged either if the maximum
    number of iterations is reached or the log probability improvement
    between the two consecutive iterations is less than threshold.
n_iter : int
    Maximum number of iterations to perform.
verbose : bool
    Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.ConvergenceMonitor._reset
documentation: Reset the monitor's state.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.ConvergenceMonitor.report
documentation:
Report convergence to {data}`sys.stderr`.

The output consists of three columns: iteration number, log
probability of the data at the current iteration and convergence
rate.  At the first iteration convergence rate is unknown and
is thus denoted by NaN.

# Parameters

log_prob

: The log probability of the data as computed by EM algorithm
  in the current iteration.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.ConvergenceMonitor.converged
documentation: Whether the EM algorithm converged.

Monitor and report convergence to {data}`sys.stderr`.

# Attributes

history

: The log probability of the data for the last two training
  iterations. If the values are not strictly increasing, the
  model did not converge.

iter

: Number of iterations performed while training the model.

# Examples

Use custom convergence criteria by subclassing `ConvergenceMonitor`
and redefining the `converged` method. The resulting subclass can
be used by creating an instance and pointing a model's `monitor_`
attribute to it prior to fitting.

```pycon
>>> from hmmlearn.base import ConvergenceMonitor
>>> from hmmlearn import hmm
>>>
>>> class ThresholdMonitor(ConvergenceMonitor):
...     @property
...     def converged(self):
...         return (self.iter == self.n_iter or
...                 self.history[-1] >= self.tol)
>>>
>>> model = hmm.GaussianHMM(n_components=2, tol=5, verbose=True)
>>> model.monitor_ = ThresholdMonitor(model.monitor_.tol,
...                                   model.monitor_.n_iter,
...                                   model.monitor_.verbose)
```

classes.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.__init__
documentation:
# Parameters

n_components

: Number of states in the model.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

random_state: RandomState or an int seed, optional

: A random number generator instance.

n_iter

: Maximum number of iterations to perform.

tol

: Convergence threshold. EM will stop if the gain in log-likelihood
  is below this value.

verbose

: Whether per-iteration convergence reports are printed to
  {data}`sys.stderr`.  Convergence can also be diagnosed using the
  {attr}`monitor_` attribute.

params, init_params

: The parameters that get updated during (`params`) or initialized
  before (`init_params`) the training.  Can contain any combination
  of 's' for startprob, 't' for transmat, and other characters for
  subclass-specific emission parameters.  Defaults to all parameters.

implementation: string, optional

: Determines if the forward-backward algorithm is implemented with
  logarithms ("log"), or using scaling ("scaling").  The default is
  to use logarithms for backwards compatability.  However, the
  scaling implementation is generally faster.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.score_samples
documentation:
Compute the log probability under the model and compute posteriors.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

log_prob

: Log likelihood of `X`.

posteriors

: State-membership probabilities for each sample in `X`.

# See Also

score : Compute the log probability under the model.
decode : Find most likely state sequence corresponding to `X`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.score
documentation:
Compute the log probability under the model.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

log_prob

: Log likelihood of `X`.

# See Also

score_samples

: posteriors.

decode : Find most likely state sequence corresponding to `X`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._score
documentation:
Helper for `score` and `score_samples`.

Compute the log probability under the model, as well as posteriors if
*compute_posteriors* is True (otherwise, an empty array is returned
for the latter).

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._score_log
documentation:
Compute the log probability under the model, as well as posteriors if
*compute_posteriors* is True (otherwise, an empty array is returned
for the latter).

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.decode
documentation:
Find most likely state sequence corresponding to `X`.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

algorithm

: Decoder algorithm.

  - "viterbi": finds the most likely sequence of states, given all
    emissions.
  - "map" (also known as smoothing or forward-backward): finds the
    sequence of the individual most-likely states, given all
    emissions.

  If not given, {attr}`decoder` is used.

# Returns

log_prob

: Log probability of the produced state sequence.

state_sequence

: Labels for each sample from `X` obtained via a given
  decoder `algorithm`.

# See Also

score_samples

: posteriors.

score : Compute the log probability under the model.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.predict
documentation:
Find most likely state sequence corresponding to `X`.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

state_sequence

: Labels for each sample from `X`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.predict_proba
documentation:
Compute the posterior probability for each state in the model.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

posteriors

: State-membership probabilities for each sample from `X`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.sample
documentation:
Generate random samples from the model.

# Parameters

n_samples

: Number of samples to generate.

random_state

: A random number generator instance. If `None`, the object's
  `random_state` is used.

currstate

: Current state, as the initial state of the samples.

# Returns

X

: Feature matrix.

state_sequence

: State sequence produced by the model.

# Examples

```
# generate samples continuously
_, Z = model.sample(n_samples=10)
X, Z = model.sample(n_samples=10, currstate=Z[-1])
```

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM.fit
documentation:
Estimate model parameters.

An initialization step is performed before entering the
EM algorithm. If you want to avoid this step for a subset of
the parameters, pass proper `init_params` keyword argument
to estimator's constructor.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._get_n_fit_scalars_per_param
documentation:
Return a mapping of fittable parameter names (as in `self.params`)
to the number of corresponding scalar parameters that will actually be
fitted.

This is used to detect whether the user did not pass enough data points
for a non-degenerate fit.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._check_sum_1
documentation: Check that an array describes one or more distributions.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._check
documentation:
Validate model parameters prior to fitting.

# Raises

ValueError

: If any of the parameters are invalid, e.g. if {attr}`startprob_`
  don't sum to 1.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._compute_likelihood
documentation:
Compute per-component probability under the model.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

# Returns

log_prob

: Log probability of each sample in `X` for each of the
  model states.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._compute_log_likelihood
documentation:
Compute per-component emission log probability under the model.

# Parameters

X

: Feature matrix of individual samples.

# Returns

log_prob

: Emission log probability of each sample in `X` for each of the
  model states, i.e., `log(p(X|state))`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._generate_sample_from_state
documentation:
Generate a random sample from a given component.

Parameters
----------
state : int
    Index of the component to condition on.
random_state: RandomState
    A random number generator instance.  (`sample` is the only caller
    for this method and already normalizes *random_state*.)

Returns
-------
X : array, shape (n_features, )
    A random sample from the emission distribution corresponding
    to a given component.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._initialize_sufficient_statistics
documentation:
Initialize sufficient statistics required for M-step.

The method is *pure*, meaning that it doesn't change the state of
the instance.  For extensibility computed statistics are stored
in a dictionary.

Returns
-------
nobs : int
    Number of samples in the data.
start : array, shape (n_components, )
    An array where the i-th element corresponds to the posterior
    probability of the first sample being generated by the i-th state.
trans : array, shape (n_components, n_components)
    An array where the (i, j)-th element corresponds to the posterior
    probability of transitioning between the i-th to j-th states.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._accumulate_sufficient_statistics
documentation:
Update sufficient statistics from a given sample.

# Parameters

stats

: Sufficient statistics as returned by
  {meth}`~.BaseHMM._initialize_sufficient_statistics`.

X

: Sample sequence.

lattice

: Probabilities OR Log Probabilities of each sample
  under each of the model states.  Depends on the choice
  of implementation of the Forward-Backward algorithm

posteriors

: Posterior probabilities of each sample being generated by each
  of the model states.

fwdlattice, bwdlattice

: forward and backward probabilities.

documentation:
Implementation of `_accumulate_sufficient_statistics`
for `implementation = "log"`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._accumulate_sufficient_statistics_log
documentation:
Implementation of `_accumulate_sufficient_statistics`
for `implementation = "log"`.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base._AbstractHMM._do_mstep
documentation:
Perform the M-step of EM algorithm.

Parameters

stats : dict
    Sufficient statistics updated from all available samples.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.BaseHMM.aic
documentation:
Akaike information criterion for the current model on the input X.

AIC = -2\*logLike + 2 * num_free_params

<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akaike_information_criterion>

# Parameters

X

: The input samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

aic

: The lower the better.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.BaseHMM.bic
documentation:
Bayesian information criterion for the current model on the input X.

BIC = -2\*logLike + num_free_params * log(num_of_data)

<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bayesian_information_criterion>

# Parameters

X

: The input samples.

lengths

: Lengths of the individual sequences in `X`. The sum of
  these should be `n_samples`.

# Returns

bic

: The lower the better.

Base class for Hidden Markov Models learned from Expectation-Maximization.

This class allows for easy evaluation of, sampling from, and maximum a
posteriori estimation of the parameters of a HMM.

Attributes
----------
monitor_ : ConvergenceMonitor
    Monitor object used to check the convergence of EM.
startprob_ : array, shape (n_components, )
    Initial state occupation distribution.
transmat_ : array, shape (n_components, n_components)
    Matrix of transition probabilities between states.

Notes
-----
Normally, one should use a subclass of `.BaseHMM`, with its specialization
towards a given emission model.  In rare cases, the base class can also be
useful in itself, if one simply wants to generate a sequence of states
using `.BaseHMM.sample`.  In that case, the feature matrix will have zero
features.

methods.name: hmmlearn.base.VariationalBaseHMM._compute_lower_bound
documentation:
Compute the Variational Lower Bound of the model as currently
configured.

Following the pattern elsewhere, derived implementations should call
this method to get the contribution of the current log_prob,
transmat, and startprob towards the lower bound

Parameters
----------
curr_logprob : float
               The current log probability of the data as computed at
               the subnormalized model parameters.

Returns
-------
lower_bound: float
             Returns the computed lower bound contribution of the
             log_prob, startprob, and transmat.
